activities_projects.title: Dimensions of Poverty Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct poverty beyond income into social and political dimensions
steps: Research the concepts of 'Social Exclusion' and 'Vulnerability' from the text
Describe how living in a poor neighborhood can lead to social exclusion from better facilities
Analyze why a Scheduled Tribe member in a remote area is more 'vulnerable' to a natural disaster than an urban worker
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes poverty as a social process rather than just a state of being
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining social exclusion and vulnerability (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Calorie vs. Consumption Expenditure
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the methods used by the NSSO to determine the poverty line
steps: Using textbook data, compare the monthly per capita expenditure (MPCE) for Rural (₹816) and Urban (₹1000) areas (2011-12 prices)
Calculate the total monthly budget for a family of 5 in both sectors
Plot a bar graph showing the price difference in essential services like transport and rent between city and village
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the economic reasoning behind different poverty thresholds
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of calculations and graphical representation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Vulnerability Matrix
activities_projects.objective: Identify the intersectionality of social groups and economic categories
steps: Create a 2x2 matrix with 'Social Groups' (SC, ST) on one axis and 'Economic Groups' (Agricultural Labour, Casual Labour) on the other
Shade the areas where the poverty ratio is highest based on the graph in the chapter
Identify the 'double disadvantage' faced by a landless laborer from a Scheduled Caste
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the concentration of poverty in specific social-economic overlaps
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct mapping of high-risk groups (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Inter-State Poverty Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Investigate why some states succeeded in reducing poverty while others failed
steps: Identify states with high poverty (Bihar, Odisha) and low poverty (Punjab, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh)
Research one specific reason for success in a low-poverty state (e.g., Land reform in West Bengal, Human resource development in Kerala)
Present a brief report on the 'Green Revolution's' role in Punjab's poverty reduction
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies diverse regional strategies for poverty alleviation
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research on state-specific success factors (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Colonial Legacy and Economic Stagnation
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the historical roots of Indian poverty
steps: Trace how the British colonial administration's policies discouraged local textiles and handicrafts
Explain the link between low economic growth in the early 20th century and high population growth
Discuss how the 'cycle of poverty' started during the colonial period
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects historical events to modern economic challenges
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow of the historical-economic argument (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Poverty Standards Debate
activities_projects.objective: Compare national poverty lines with international benchmarks
steps: Compare the Indian poverty line with the World Bank’s $1.90 a day (PPP) standard
Discuss why a 'minimum subsistence' level of living is different from a 'reasonable' level of living
Analyze why poverty appears to decrease faster in China than in Sub-Saharan Africa
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates poverty as a relative and absolute concept globally
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical analysis of global versus local standards (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Evaluating Anti-Poverty Programs
activities_projects.objective: Assess the effectiveness of targeted government interventions
steps: Create a comparative table of three schemes: MGNREGA, PMGY (Pradhan Mantri Gramodaya Yojana), and REGP (Rural Employment Generation Programme)
Identify the 'Target Group' for each scheme
List two major reasons mentioned in the text for the lack of complete success of these programs (e.g., poor implementation, overlapping)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically examines the administrative challenges of welfare schemes
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed comparison and identification of implementation gaps (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Population and Poverty Nexus
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how population growth impacts per capita income
steps: Simulate a scenario: A village income grows by 5%, but the population also grows by 5%
Calculate the change in 'Per Capita Income' and explain why poverty remains unchanged
Discuss how high population growth leads to the 'fragmentation of land' among poor families
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the math behind the 'vicious cycle' of population and poverty
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct mathematical simulation and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Wealth Inequality Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Explore the role of land and asset distribution in poverty
steps: Read about the 'unequal distribution of land' in the chapter
Explain how lack of land forces rural poor into low-paying casual labor
Discuss how 'Small Farmers' get trapped in debt because of the high cost of farming inputs (seeds, fertilizers)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates asset ownership to economic security
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of understanding regarding rural debt and landlessness (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Broadening the Definition: Human Poverty
activities_projects.objective: Conceptually move from 'Income Poverty' to 'Human Poverty'
steps: Define 'Human Poverty' using the text's concluding thoughts
List five non-monetary elements: Education, Shelter, Healthcare, Gender Equality, and Dignity
Create a 'Human Poverty Index' checklist for an imaginary village to measure their true well-being
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes a holistic view of human development and rights
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and comprehensiveness of the checklist (10 marks)
